title: quercus faginea var. fagifolia
intro: Quercus faginea var. fagifolia, also known as the Portuguese oak, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Quercus genus, native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Quercus faginea var. fagifolia include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and salves for various health issues.
